Completes the **orchestrator-ownership half** of the temporal-pyramid aggregation lane — the durable L1 (session) / L2 (daily) tiers of the ADR 0028 substrate, built on the #14897 scaffold.

> **Draft — AC2 + AC3 land here; AC4–AC8 (enablement completeness) follow on this branch.** `aggregationEnabled` stays `false` until every AC passes (AC1), so the lane is inert by construction until then.

Resolves #14938
Refs #14434 · Related: #12679 · #14811

## Deltas

Five commits, engine → service → orchestrator → cleanup:

1. **Engine L1 planner** (`e90ef16c4b`) — `resolveSessionWindow` / `planSessionWindows`: the hour-aligned L1 (session) tier mirroring the L2 daily pair. `level:'session'` is a sub-daily **clock** window — the metadata contract keys every tier by `{windowStart, windowEnd}` with no sessionId — so each L1 window nests within exactly one L2 day (no cross-window double-count).
2. **Service L1/L2 planning** (`f30c61c9dd`) — `collectPendingWindows` now plans **both** tiers each cycle, each tagged with its `level` so `runCycle` mints the matching `SUMMARY_SESSION` / `SUMMARY_DAILY` record (was L2-only).
3. **Orchestrator registration** (`0eb9b01259`) — a `temporal-summary` `TASK_REGISTRY` descriptor (in-process-async / heavy / exclusive-heavy) **and** classification in the `MaintenanceBackpressureService` heavy-SSOT, so exclusive-heavy mutual exclusion holds both as-candidate and as-running.
4. **Dispatch wiring** (`b68ee71793`) — an in-process runner drives `runCycle` under the heavy-maintenance lease (`executeWithMaintenance` → `acquireLeaseAndExecute`); `buildOrchestratorSchedulingOptions` wires cadence / enable / service, reading `AiConfig.temporalSummary.*` at the use site (ADR-0019, no threading).
5. **Poll-loop retirement** (`7782a27104`) — removed the service's standalone `start/stop/scheduleNext/pulse` + `acquireLease/releaseLease` loop, plus `daemon.mjs` and the `ai:temporal-summary` npm script (−405 lines). The orchestrator is now the **sole** scheduler — the "no independent setInterval beside the orchestrator" contract.

## Contract Ledger

| Consumed surface | Consumer | Change | Compatibility |
|---|---|---|---|
| `TASK_REGISTRY` | orchestrator collector / picker | +1 `temporal-summary` descriptor | additive; inert while `enables.temporalSummary` is unset/false |
| `DEFAULT_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_TASK_NAMES` | `isHeavyMaintenanceTask` | +`temporal-summary` | additive; the SSOT-pin test updated in lockstep |
| `buildOrchestratorSchedulingOptions` (intervals / enables / services) | scheduling pipeline | +`temporalSummary` cadence / enable / service | additive; reads `AiConfig.temporalSummary.*` at the use site |
| `TemporalSummaryAggregationService` | orchestrator (was: `daemon.mjs`) | poll-loop + lease methods removed; `runCycle()` is the sole entry | `daemon.mjs` + `ai:temporal-summary` script retired |
| `AiConfig.temporalSummary.aggregationEnabled` | orchestrator enable gate | now consumed by the orchestrator (was the standalone daemon) | unchanged leaf; stays `false` (AC1) |

Evidence: every increment was verified green in isolation before commit; the removed lease/backpressure behavior did not disappear — it moved to the orchestrator layer and is covered by the pipeline dispatch test below.

## Test Evidence

- `temporalSummaryAggregationEngine.spec` — **26/26** (L1 planner: half-open hour bounds, day-boundary rollover, fail-closed anchor, bounded most-recent-first batch, L1-nests-in-L2 coherence).
- `TemporalSummaryAggregationService.spec` — **14/14** (L1/L2 window planning + trailing-window defaults; the 7 poll-loop/lease tests were removed with their methods).
- `registry.spec` — **17/17** (temporal-summary descriptor: disabled / unset-enable / due / within-cadence + heavy-SSOT parity).
- `MaintenanceBackpressureService.spec` — the heavy-SSOT pin updated + green. ⚠️ The `acquireLeaseAndExecute` / `resolveHeavyMaintenanceLeasePath` lease-file-I/O tests fail **pre-existing** in this worktree — stash-verified on clean HEAD, logically independent of the heavy-set membership change (they never reference `temporal-summary`).
- `pipeline.spec` — **23/23** (+2: `buildOrchestratorSchedulingOptions` temporal-summary wiring; `executeCandidate` heavy-lease dispatch → `runCycle`).

## Post-Merge Validation

Deferred to AC7/AC8 (this draft lands AC2 + AC3 only): one real Chroma upsert, one idempotent replay, and one orchestrator-owned execution — all **before** flipping `aggregationEnabled`. Until then the lane is inert by construction: the `enables.temporalSummary` guard fails closed, so no window is ever planned, dispatched, or written.

## Remaining on this branch (before `ready_for_review`)

- **AC4** — `sandboxesGraduated` from exact `[GRADUATED_TO_TICKET: #N]` author-actions + their event timestamps (paginate the full conversation, reject marker-like prose, fail closed on absent evidence).
- **AC5** — `SUMMARY_DAILY` graph label written only by this deterministic lane + the ADR 0024 §2.2 node-type-table update in the same PR (the pre-declared §2.7 obligation).
- **AC6** — append-only version progression + bounded retention.
- **AC7 / AC8** — full focused test matrix (success / deferral / pagination / partial-source / write-failure / replay / retention) + live durable-write validation.
